title: The old radio in my kitchen
summary: Describe an old radio that plays in your kitchen every morning.
topic: The old radio in my kitchen
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about an old radio in your kitchen. Describe what it looks like, when you turn it on, and what you listen to. Use present simple.
hintStarters: There is an old radio on my kitchen shelf. It is…
sampleText: There is an old radio on my kitchen shelf in Buenos Aires. The radio is a small brown wooden box with two round buttons. One button is for the volume and one is for the dial. The radio is older than I am. I bought it at a second-hand shop near the bus station for very little money. I turn the radio on every morning when I make coffee. I listen to the morning news for ten minutes, and then I turn the dial to a small music station. The music is soft, often piano or guitar. The sound is warm and a little dry, not clear like my phone. The radio sometimes makes a small noise when a bus passes outside. I do not mind. In winter, I listen to the weather report so I know what to wear. The kitchen feels alive when the radio plays. An old radio is a small friend in the morning.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một người bạn nhỏ vào buổi sáng

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Comparative 'older than'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Với tính từ ngắn (một âm tiết), thêm -er và 'than': 'The radio is older than I am'. So sánh hai vật, hai người.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: With short adjectives (one syllable), add -er + 'than': 'The radio is older than I am'. Used to compare two things or people.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Phrasal verbs: 'turn on', 'turn the dial'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'turn on' nghĩa là bật: 'I turn the radio on'. 'turn the dial' nghĩa là vặn núm. Tân ngữ ngắn có thể đặt giữa hoặc sau phrasal verb.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'turn on' means to switch on: 'I turn the radio on'. 'turn the dial' means to rotate the knob. A short object can go between or after the phrasal verb.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một cái đài cũ trên kệ bếp nhà tôi ở Buenos Aires. Đài là một chiếc hộp gỗ nâu nhỏ với hai núm tròn. Một núm dùng để chỉnh âm lượng và một núm để dò sóng. Cái đài còn lớn tuổi hơn tôi. Tôi mua nó ở một tiệm đồ cũ gần bến xe buýt với rất ít tiền. Tôi bật đài mỗi sáng khi pha cà phê. Tôi nghe tin tức buổi sáng mười phút, sau đó tôi vặn núm sang một đài nhạc nhỏ. Nhạc nhẹ, thường là piano hoặc guitar. Âm thanh ấm và hơi khô, không trong như điện thoại của tôi. Cái đài đôi khi phát ra tiếng nhỏ khi xe buýt chạy qua bên ngoài. Tôi không phiền. Vào mùa đông, tôi nghe bản tin thời tiết để biết mặc gì. Căn bếp như sống động khi đài phát. Một cái đài cũ là một người bạn nhỏ vào buổi sáng.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'There is an old radio on my kitchen shelf'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the location in the first sentence.

mcQuestions.prompt: How many buttons does the radio have?
Where did the writer buy the radio?
options: At a second-hand shop near the bus station
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I bought it at a second-hand shop near the bus station'.

mcQuestions.prompt: When does the writer turn the radio on?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I turn the radio on every morning when I make coffee'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long does the writer listen to the morning news?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I listen to the morning news for ten minutes'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the duration of the news.

mcQuestions.prompt: What kind of music plays after the news?
options: Soft music, often piano or guitar
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The music is soft, often piano or guitar'.

options: The radio is more old than I am.
The radio is older than I am.
The radio is more older than I am.
The radio is most old than I am.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why does the writer listen to the weather report in winter?
options: So the writer knows what to wear
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I listen to the weather report so I know what to wear'.

options: I turn on it every morning.
I turn it the radio on.
I turn the radio on every morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: Short noun objects can go between 'turn' and 'on': 'turn the radio on'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the correct word order for a phrasal verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the writer describe the radio at the end?
options: A small friend in the morning
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'An old radio is a small friend in the morning'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do some people enjoy old objects more than new, perfect ones? What can an old radio give that a phone speaker cannot?
